item.title: How AI guardrails are impeding the work of offensive cybersecurity researchers
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: We spoke with several cybersecurity researchers, who look for unknown vulnerabilities and develop tools to exploit them, about how OpenAI’s and Anthropic’s guardrails affect their work.

item.title: AMD takes on Nvidia with its Helios AI rack-scale system
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: AMD is challenging its chipmaker rival with a new rack-scale system that will start shipping to customers later this year.

item.title: Anthropic updates Claude voice mode with more capable models
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Claude's new voice model will let you reschedule your meeting or draft an email.

item.title: AegisAI, founded by former Google security execs, lands $36M to stop AI-driven spear phishing
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: AegisAI co-founders developed AI agents that quickly analyze each message as a human would, paying attention to small anomalies that even the most elaborate checklist wouldn’t catch.

item.title: Runway launches AI model router as generative media gets crowded
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: The Media Router is a tool that automatically selects the best image, video, or audio generation model for a request based on whether a developer prioritizes quality, speed or cost.

item.title: OpenAI makes ChatGPT Health available to all US users
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Users can also integrate their personal data from services like Apple Health, Function, and MyFitnessPal.

item.title: Meta launched a new AI optimism ad set to a song about human extinction
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: David Bowie's song "Five Years," which Meta used in a supposedly inspiring advertisement, is about humans learning that they have five years left to live before the apocalypse.

item.title: Nvidia is sending GPUs to the moon
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: If there's a place in the universe without GPUs, Nvidia is sending them there.

item.title: AI chip startup Etched defies skeptics, hits $10.3B valuation from big-name investors
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Etched, founded by three Harvard dropouts, has created new chips and memory components that speed up inference on any AI model -- no GPUs required, it says.

item.title: Google’s Gemini nears billion-user milestone
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Gemini had over 750 million monthly users in February.

item.title: Experts say exploiting Anthropic’s Fable isn’t how Kimi K3 got so good
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: "I don't think you get a model this strong and this quickly on the heels of Fable doing strictly distillation," one expert told TechCrunch.

item.title: ServiceNow bets $40 million on Indian banking software specialist to expand its financial services push
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: ServiceNow's investment gives BusinessNext a strategic partner to expand its AI-powered banking software globally.

item.title: After shocking quarter, IBM insists that AI isn’t killing the mainframe
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: After IBM's stock crashed last week on warnings of poor mainframe sales, the CEO explained that AI wrecked corporate hardware budget, temporarily.

item.title: Google justifies its massive AI spending with a booming cloud business
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Google's cloud business is thriving, as companies adopting its AI and AI infrastructure services help the tech giant to report record profits.

item.title: Treasury threatens sanctions after White House claims Moonshot distilled Anthropic’s Fable
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: The episode has also intensified a broader debate in Washington over the influx of Chinese open models.

item.title: How OpenAI’s human mistake led to the AI-powered hack on Hugging Face
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: OpenAI made a mistake setting up what it called a “highly isolated” testing environment and sandbox. According to cybersecurity experts, that human mistake is what made the AI-powered attack on Hugging Face possible.

item.title: Travis Kalanick’s robotics company raises $1.7B, led by a16z
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: Uber is also investing in Travis Kalanick's company Atoms, which has made gauzy claims about using industrial AI to modernize the world.

item.title: Yope raises $12.3M to build a private social network without algorithms or ads
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Yope, a fast-growing social app focused on private groups of friends and family, has raised $12.3 million in seed funding. Instead of chasing creators and algorithmic feeds, the startup is betting that the future of social networking lies in small, private communities powered by messaging, photo sharing, and AI features designed to strengthen real-world relationships.

item.title: Monday.com lays off hundreds to focus on AI
item.dc:creator: Ram Iyer
item.description: The company said it is reducing its headcount by 20%, or about 630 staff, to "support a leaner, more focused operating model" as it focuses on its AI Work Platform.

item.title: Arcee, a US open source AI lab, says Chinese models are not inherently dangerous
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: As Chinese AI models grow in capability and popularity among U.S. companies, the arguing over what should be done about them has reached a fever pitch.
